mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, mechanically compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals labor market slack and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that depress S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when unemployment rises rapidly over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer price inflation and record-high household savings indicate a structural shift in consumption patterns and monetary drag, which suppresses corporate earnings growth and yields negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term shows no predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening real economic activity, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO growth (t-stat < 1.5) across out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals weakening demand in interest-sensitive sectors and broader economic deceleration, forecasting negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Housing declines do not precede negative equity returns within 60 trading days in >70% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation combined with historically low policy rates creates stagflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and trigger equity sell-offs over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral during high-inflation/low-rate periods, yielding a negative or null Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns with Rank IC < 0.01 across all test periods.
